title: dioscorea hastata var. hassleriana
intro: Dioscorea hastata var. hassleriana, also known as the Hassler's yam, is a type of yam plant that belongs to the Dioscoreaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is believed to have a number of health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving circulation, and relieving pain and swelling. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often made from the roots, tubers, or leaves of the plant. Additionally, the plant's medicinal properties are thought to be attributed to its high levels of saponins and other bioactive compounds, which are also found in other medicinal plants within the Dioscoreaceae family.
